reviews.review_description: Alpaca Party was way more than I expected. The graphics are cool and the play is smooth. With. The ever changing maps, veteran players don’t have such a big advantage over newer players. I played this game early on so there weren’t always too many players to team up with. It’s a lot of fun. I recommend playing this one.

reviews.review_title: be carefull dont break the glass
reviews.review_description:
if you want to party with friend's in a strange maze network as alpaca this is really the place to be 
the momentum to liftup object's and collect is great done also great sound but some are scary one of monster looking around for killing you so you are warned about this to and find the bos to end the game in all glory ;-))

reviews.review_title: This game is so freaking good
reviews.review_description: To start the game is original it foes not copy from any game it is inspired in repo tho but the bedt part is the monster since they are very different and not the same thing over and over again also i would let yall know, please leave a review a very honest one, pssst... join alpaca discord for giveaways👀

If you enjoy games like Repo you will have fun with friends and/or random people in this.
The physics feel good in VR and you want to be careful not to break valuables.
You can respawn in levels as long as another team mate is alive before the timer runs out which makes it feel a bit faster pace and less downtime than similar games.

This is a fairly standard repo style game where you run around collecting loot while avoiding monsters. I was hoping it would introduce some fresh ideas or do something to move the genre forward, but it doesn’t really offer anything unique. It’s a solid entry point for newcomers, but veterans of the genre will likely find it repetitive and uninspired.

Honestly, this game is a blast. If you like Lethal Company, you’re gonna love this. Pushing the cart around with friends and trying not to get wiped out by monsters while grabbing loot is pure chaos. The physics are actually really good and the respawn mechanic keeps it from being annoying when you die.

My only real gripes are that it’s basically impossible to enjoy solo. They really need to add a practice or single-player mode so new players can actually learn how to play without getting thrown straight into the wolves. Also, even though there’s barely any text, it’s only in English right now. Adding localized text would help a ton for global players. Still, totally worth downloading if you have a group to play with!

reviews.review_title: Who's up for a little party?
reviews.review_description: Elements of this game feel like a work in progress, but since it is supposed to be a little chaotic, it's also forgiving as a party game - and believe me, you want to play well with others.  Going alone can be brutal.  All-round this game is fun.  It's 'free' to give it a red hot go, so just go for it.

I genuinely love this game. It is super fun to play with friends or just the community. Mostly all of them community is super kind. If you like REPO or Deadly Delivery, you will love this. I can recommend it completely.

This was a fun and entertaining experience with similar playstyle as repo.  You go around different levels collecting loot and treasures to earn money.  You then spend that money on tools to combat the monsters easier.  Careful though some objects can break others while dropping or hitting things damage the value of the loot.  The levels are pretty fun to explore with a certain level of creepiness to the game.  The monsters are either kinda creepy or pretty funny looking.    I'd like to have the ability to do private lobbies with friends as a few times random squeezed into the group.  Solo is doable at least to a point but alot more fun and easier with others.  I'll keep checking back with this one as it does have a few issues needing ironed out.

reviews.review_title: Free but still rough around the edges
reviews.review_description:
This is tough to review because it's free, and it's hard to say a free game isn't worth playing (unless that game was a virus or bitminer).  Since it's free, it's at least worth trying out.  I do still think this game needs a lot of time to be on par with others in the genre and I think the devs should start charging $5 or so for it when it is that good (unless they think paid cosmetics is enough).  

The trailer doesn't make me want to play.  Enemies are almost all avoided by just waiting, then walking away as they attack (even the new pipe girl feels identical to the knife girl, imo).  The shops don't always offer power-ups, which is rough for solo.  The public lobby system is frustrating because I don't want to play with screaming children.  There is no smooth turning.  Item physics is more chaotic than REPO.  The list goes on and on (including bugs).  I see the potential for this, and I really want to see it grow, but this is the first game in the Lethal Company genre where I not only survived insanely long solo, but also quit because I was just bored.  I'll do my best to update my review as the game grows and, honestly, just adding private (and solo) lobbies along with fixing the enemies to be actually unique and interesting would bump it to a 4-star for me. Right now, even at the cost of free, this is "meh".

reviews.review_title: A social game that Alpacas potential
reviews.review_description:
Alpaca Party is a light grab and go exploration game with a fairly fun rouge-like loop: enter a dungeon areas, avoid or fight monsters, gather loot and return enough of it to a recycler in order to clear the level, take too long and more monsters will spawn (which can either help you gather the items you need or finish you off if ill-equipped). Every third level spawns a boss at the end. 

Overall I was pleasantly surprised by this little game, the graphics are nothing to write home about, but the loop can be fun, especially with others. I had the most fun playing with other people, as a solo experience, it felt kinda lonely and the areas are large enough that it feel less like exploring and more like trudging in search of treasure. 

I do like the mechanism where there are breakable objects so handle some with care, though I find that the grip and storage controls could use some refining as it was too easy to grab treasure only to drop and break it instead of placing it in storage. 

As it currently stands I rate this game as a 3-star experience, but closer to 4-stars if you can play with others, check it out, there is a lot of potential and I look forward to improving the score over time.

As all the reviews say, it’s just a repo copy.
I was disappointed in its current state, but I think if this game tried to pick up some of its own identity and did a lot of polishing to fix a lot of the difficult mechanics, and balancing, it could be fun and do well.
I know that I’ve had fun using a repo vr mod on the real version, but this game does not even come close to scratching that itch currently
Hoping this game gets better with updates, and I’ll check it out again every once in a while and updates my review accordingly.

I guess saying its like R.E.P.O. Is the easiest way to get across what the game is, but it does a couple things different.
It has similarities like the fragile loot with some having special effects, you get a cart to push around and carry loot to extration points while evading (or killing) monsters and buying gadgets at the shop afterwards. Its honestly easier to explain what it does different.
The biggest difference is that youre on a timer. Does running out of time make you lose? No, and im not sure what exactly happens, but a part of it is respawns not being available anymore.
Respawns? Yeah, when you die you can respawn at the start of the map and suprisingly quickly at that. If everyone is dead its game over though, so playing in a group is immensely easier than solo.
This changes the pace from slow and careful like in REPO, and turns it into a rush to get everything and leave.

It’s pretty much like repo except your an alpaca. There are a few differences like your on a time limit and you can respawn if other players are still alive but that’s about it. I’ll be honest it’s not in a great state as of this review but I do have hope that it can become an amazing game.

reviews.review_title: Feels like your average F2P game
reviews.review_description:
Textures look very low quality, audio sounds like it was pulled from public archives, mechanics are not explained well and are based off you already understanding how its 'inspired by' game plays (REPO).

If you've never played REPO, like me, this game ends up feeling empty and hard to find fun in. Grab mechanics are mildly inconsistent and loot is not clearly explained on what needs 3+ hands to pick up.

I have proof that this game is Repo and here is my proof you click grip to grab and. The. PC type of repo you click grip two and you have to gain money to get or should i say make money anyway i don't know if you noticed. There is a cart for money and objects and the PC repo has a cart for money. And that's my proof

If you're into games like REPO or Deadly Delivery this is a similar game that is at the same time quite different. It being free to play means you should just check it out and see if it's for you. It has different mechanics that seem to be thought for VR specifically, it can be quite fun.

At first, I was going to leave a negative review, but that was when I was playing solo and didn’t know what to do. Once I joined a party, the game became much more fun, and I actually understood what I was supposed to do.

There were bugs here and there, but I’m sure the developers will fix them, and the game will improve in the near future. The controller movement is okay, but it still needs work.

The main objective is to extract valuable objects so you can upgrade yourself and even gain access to weapons if you want. It’s very fun to play. Some bugs can get in the way, and a few even prevent you from killing creatures, which is weird. Overall, though, I highly recommend this game.

Grab & Run Alpaca Party VR is a wildly fun and chaotic party game that really shines in multiplayer. You and up to three friends play as alpacas, scrambling to collect loot and escape dangerous monsters. The gameplay is fast-paced and intense, your heart will be racing as you try to safely haul valuables back to the recycler without damaging them along the way.

One of the standout mechanics is how teamwork is built into the experience. Some loot is too heavy to carry alone, so you’ll need to coordinate with others to move it efficiently. If you’re not careful and drop it into the cart, you can lose value, which adds a nice layer of strategy and tension.

The monsters keep things interesting as well. Each one behaves differently and requires a bit of thought to defeat. Smashing them into the ground feels incredibly satisfying, but it also leaves you vulnerable, so you always need to stay aware of your surroundings.

While the game can be played solo, it’s significantly more challenging and clearly designed with multiplayer in mind. Since it’s still in early access, you may encounter some bugs, but it’s clear the developers are actively improving the experience.

Overall, this game has a ton of potential and already delivers a chaotic, hilarious, and teamwork-driven VR experience that’s especially great with friends.

reviews.review_title: It's good, but not great.... Yet
reviews.review_description: Now I only really have two gripes with this game, no private matches and the tutorial can be skipped with no way to get back to it. That being said the game is hilarious and fun! It really is just like repo but in VR! The monsters are cool and all have their own unique mechanics, the gameplay is fluid and smooth. I think that once they fix the matchmaking to have private servers (if they do) this game will be a go to for future VR goers who want that repo experience.

This is a good game but -wait I actually have two things. Anyway I like it but one of the monsters I found in the first lvl (the one that crawls on the ground) is too easy and doesn’t have much health and the other monster (the big red one) has too much health and you need like three people tho kill it and it still takes like 15 mins, secondly there aren’t enough players I only met one person but it’s a good game just it would be more fun with more players. Good job devs

reviews.review_title: Social Extraction Adventure made for VR
reviews.review_description: The title of the game says it all: you are playing as an alpaca, running through spooky mazes in search for heavy treasures to grab and sell against an unforgiving countdown. If you are familiar with Lethal Company, Murky Divers or R.E.P.O.,  you get the same brew here in VR - but VR mechanics are great with smooth gameplay and various locomotion options (in different sizes even). Preferably played together with forgiving Paco friends. But keep in mind: some alpacas can be explosive!

This game is a lot of fun and would be a great start to anyones VR journey. It has some lightly scary enemies and plenty of items, weapons and traps to play around with. I think this would be a good choice for anyone looking for a game to play with their kids that will be engaging for both age groups. The matchmaking system is super quick and the runs are easy to jump into without a huge time commitment.

reviews.review_title: A mix of cute and scary
reviews.review_description: The game is split into multiple levels and a boss level. In each level, you have to reach a certain amount of loot. I really enjoyed the game. There are parts of the game that are not playable solo, so the game is more enjoyable if you've got friends. Joining randoms is fine as well, but I would recommend you get a group of friends and play the game. I love the customization and the character design. The gameplay is also fun, not too difficult and not too easy.

reviews.review_title: Easy to understand, hard to master
reviews.review_description:
A chaotic, physics-driven multiplayer VR game that blends party gameplay with light roguelike/extraction mechanics. You and your friends play as slightly unhinged alpacas, diving into procedurally generated maps, grabbing loot, and trying to escape while monsters interfere at every turn.

The game follows a simple but effective loop. You enter the maze, grab valuables, survive monsters (eliminate them), extract, put everything in the recycler, go back to the ship, and buy some weapons….

Everything has weight, so carrying loot becomes hilariously clumsy rather than efficient.

The game clearly shines when played cooperatively. It is easy to understand, hard to master.

It’s an excellent social VR experience: chaos + teamwork = lots of laughs.

I don’t recommend it to play solo.

Even though I haven’t played the original repo, this game is a very good substitute for it’s randomly generated rooms. Also the environment fits the style of  a VR Repo game. The only thing I will say is that when you have to lift a heavy object it is pretty tricky to keep it up, but other than that it’s a very good game.

This is a fun multiplayer game similar to Repo. You have to enter buildings with your friends and you have to collect loot without dying. It is very enjoyable but more variety and overall polish would be appreciated.

reviews.review_title: Great potential, just needs some fine tu
reviews.review_description:
The game has a solid foundation, and you can really tell the devs put love into it. Multiplayer is genuinely fun and chaotic. But it's still early access, so I gotta complain a little bit.

The main issue is the controls feel a bit floaty. Take the tutorial level for example. If the on screen prompt hadn't told me, I never would have thought to move that obstacle box. And because the grab feels kinda floaty, I ended up wrestling with that box for a long time before I finally figured out how to move it.

One more thing: please add a single player mode with AI teammates. Sometimes there's no one in the lobby, and it would be nice to at least have a bot with me.

My bigger concern right now is whether the long term reward loop will hold up. I'm not sure if the devs will stay motivated to keep polishing this down the road. Still, I'm leaving a positive review to encourage them. This game has great potential. Looking forward to future updates.

The game is missing smooth turning, and it needs more optimisations. I liked that I could physically interact with enemies to defeat them. One other nitpick: It can be hard to navigate, if you are not using a map.
kp
Yes! it's a great game and it's also a good experience. Sorry that's all i got to say but I recommend playing the game!

I would give this 5/5 but I don't have any players to play with. It is pretty fun and level design is funny. It is pretty unique game there is plenty to do and every single run is different.

I would play this more If I could get friends
This is repo but in vr great experience

me like gam,
 game good
I'm just doing this for the coat
Great extraction game with different mechanics. Very approachable for newcomers and veterans alike. Game always being updated and continues to improve as time goes on

reviews.review_description: Cool
Great game the recycler just doesn't seem to work
It stoped werking after the first time

Hi jjellis.2023,

Sorry to hear the game stopped working after your first session, that's definitely not what we want anyone to experience.

We're still in early access with a small team, so occasional issues can slip through, but we're actively fixing them and would love to help you out directly. Could you hop into our Discord and look for "AK" or post in the help channel? Our team can work with you 1 on 1 to figure out what's going on and hopefully get you back into the game.

reviews.review_description: I’m not saying this just because I was apart of their beta testing, this game is scary fun! The game concept is real simple. You’re a cute but kindof creepy looking alpaca, team up with others or play solo, and collect vases worth month spread out on floors. Collecting enough money allows purchasing of upgrades after completion of each floor because..you have to AVOID or FIGHT monsters while doing so! Each monsters have unique mechanics or strategies so do your homework! Did I mention this game is FREE?! No brainer to download and give this game a try!

reviews.review_title: this game is just like repo
reviews.review_description: this game is exactly like repo but for strangers to meet each other without having to have friends

reviews.review_title: A diamond in the rough (EA review)
reviews.review_description:
In a world where there are a myriad of GTag clones dominating the free to play charts.....comes this little diamond in the rough.

The game is a little rough around the edges but its to be expected from an EA release.
Don't let this put you off as the game is set to get better as the dev expands and polishes the game.

Like R.E.P.O the aim is to explore biomes that are randomly generated and gather enough loot to meet the quota.
You have to be a bit careful with the fragile loot as it can lose value and eventually break if you aren't careful enough.....sounds simple?

Well this is where the monsters comes in!
Each one has its own unique quirks that you have to learn and overcome if you want to hit quota.
Some have some interesting behaviors and it will be interesting to the way the developer will mislead and surprise players expectations! (In how they attack or behave)
Of course there are plenty of Alpacas......of the exploding variety!

All hope is not lost as players can take advantage of the available tools that the players can buy between rounds to give then a slight edge.

A promising start for this VR entry in the "friendslop genre" that deserves attention from the sea of G-tag clones.

Sure it could do with a little bit more polish and a few more enemies and biomes but this is just start for the title!
I can't wait to see how the dev expand on the title in the future. (Especially as the dev are prepared to break from the R.E.P.O template and do its own thing - like bosses)

Best to of all its free to play! So there's noting to lose really.

The game characters are very cute, and the controls are simple. The bosses are a bit scary—it’s best to play with friends, which makes things much easier. However, there are some issues: 
1. You can’t play without an internet connection. 
2. There is no gameplay guide. 
3. It’s unclear how to defeat the monsters. New players will feel lost at the beginning. 
4. Playing solo can be very troublesome.

reviews.review_title: An interesting take on a fun formula.
reviews.review_description:
In this game, your aim is in the same as R.E.P.O.

However, I personally felt like the overall gameplay was SO MUCH BETTER since the translation into VR was pretty much seemless.

I also like the original monsters too that are present in the game as all of their designs are completely original.

What also was nice was the controls too. I personally quite like when games throw players into unfamiliar situations in horror situations. Since this games controls are alot different from the average games controls, it makes for a thrilling experience if you havent played any other alpaca-games from the studio.

I struggled to find anything wrong with the game as tbh, it was a perfect translation for something that we ALL love.

Five Stars - ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
I played with my sister and there are so many glitches and i die to nothing this game is honestly buns

developer_response.body:
Hi Caids09,

Sorry to hear you and your sister had a rough time, that's definitely not the experience we want anyone to have. Dying to nothing and running into glitches sounds super frustrating, especially when you're just trying to have fun together.

We're still in early access with a small team, so bugs can slip through. But we're actively fixing them and would love to help you out directly. Could you hop into our Discord and look for "AK" or post in the help channel? Our team can work with you 1-on-1 to figure out what's going on and hopefully get you two a better experience.

Also, if you remember what headset you were using, that'd help us track down the issue faster.

Thanks for giving us a shot anyway, feedback like yours helps us improve.

Cheers,
The Alpaca Party Dev Team

reviews.review_description:
Atmosphere is on pointThe abandoned world vibe is sick. Monsters actually scare me. Just wish they were slightly smarter and spawned a bit less. Still hooked
Saw clips on tiktok, downloaded it, now I'm addicted. Simple concept executed well. Items bug out occasionally but whatever, it's free

The core gameplay of this title is really interesting—a repo-like style fits VR perfectly and has huge immersion potential. But as an Early Access version, there are still many issues that need optimization ASAP: First, about the monsters: Currently, they aren’t very intimidating or smart. Most importantly, they spawn way too frequently—they feel as common as stray dogs rather than sources of unknown terror. Sometimes you can even see one just standing in the distance, which breaks the tension since they should appear more stealthily. Combined with overly flat and empty map design, being chased just means running across open ground with nowhere to hide or maneuver, missing the intended nervous. Also, I suggest hiding the monster kill count to increase player pressure. Controls: The grab detection needs tweaking—items often break for no clear reason. Also, adjusting object distance is currently button-based, which is unintuitive and clunky. Strongly recommend changing it to stick push/pull. Comfort: Only comfort turning is available, no smooth turning—this feels really awkward. You often end up in awkward angles and have to play with your head tilted. Hope smooth turning is added soon. Sound: The current sound effects feel rough and don’t pull you into the experience. Overall, the gameplay foundation is strong, but the current experience still feels rough. Looking forward to future updates.

This VR game Alpaca Party exceeded my expectations. As an Early Access version, it already feels quite polished. Playing as an alpaca and collecting items in randomly generated maps feels great—the physics are solid, especially the sense of weight when handling objects. The monsters each have their own characteristics, requiring observation of their patterns, which adds strategy. The multiplayer co-op mode makes the experience more fun, and the fact that it’s currently free shows real sincerity. The comfort settings are reasonable, and I didn’t experience any discomfort during short sessions. Looking forward to future updates—I’ll keep an eye on this one.

I was very excited before playing, but the current content feels somewhat lacking. First, the early game is way too difficult，players have no means to fight back, and monsters can pass through walls unimpeded. The map generation logic is also strange, with too many dead ends and few hiding spots, making it hard to escape or evade. Second, the controls feel off—comfort turning sometimes causes items to drop when you turn, and in multiplayer, throwing objects makes them fly around unpredictably. Looking forward to the developer’s improvements.

The gameplay is quite good, but there are a few points that need polishing. For example, the latency occasionally spikes unexpectedly, and there’s an issue with the grab/snap system，when two players try to pick up the same item, it tends to wobble or behave erratically. Additionally, monsters spawn too frequently and have too much health, while there are too few hiding spots. During combat, you often just end up running around aimlessly. Looking forward to the developer making improvements.

The dual movement options can be switched freely, making positioning and movement flexible and smooth—the immersion is absolutely top-notch. The only downside is the lack of anti-motion-sickness settings, which causes slight dizziness, though it eases after a short adjustment. The core gameplay follows the currently super popular "loot-and-battle" style, and with VR’s physical realism, the early game—when you have few attack options—feels especially thrilling. Exploring the environment or running into opponents keeps you on edge, making you tread carefully the whole time. The tension is real. The game also supports 4-player online co-op, and teaming up with friends to loot and progress together has great atmosphere. The cooperative interaction doubles the fun. Once you figure out the strategies later on and unlock/buy various items, you basically become an unstoppable force. Anyone else who loves this game—add me and let’s team up!

I’ve been looking forward to a VR version ever since playing the company's earlier games, and now I’ve finally tried it. The immersive experience really makes the environment and atmosphere more realistic. The controls take some getting used to due to hardware limitations, but that doesn’t stop this type of game from being really fun. I’m really looking forward to future developments, like more types of pets and more maps.
Scary fun with friendsPlayed with a group last night and we were dying laughing Monster spawn rate is a tad high and physics can be janky sometimes but for free? Can't complain

reviews.review_title: Lots of Potential, Not quite there yet.
reviews.review_description:
Like many other reviews, I will point out that this is, essentially, Repo with alpacas. The game is alright, throwing the monsters around can be fun, and I really enjoy the movment particularly with the weapons (try jumping and shooting a shotgun) but my main problems in the game as it currently stands are 3 different issues.

1: On startup (Quest 3) sometimes the game just flashes repeatedly for around 30-40 seconds and sometimes indefinitely. The game is early access, so not the worst thing but still a big problem when there are some stability issues. 

2: The monsters. Im not sure if this was an issue on my end, but they didn't seem to have much, if any audio cues in any way, except for a music sting that would sometimes start if the monster is in the same room as you. This is problematic, because without any kind of audio queue that there is a monster nearby, the just kills you monster can kill you with little to no counterplay. 

3: The cart. It has a few problems, which sucks because it is the primary way you move around loot around the map. I have 2-3 different issues with the cart right now. Currently there is no or maybe reduced collision damage in the cart, which is unfortunate because the game is snap movement for head movement which I have had more than one occasion where i turn my joystick and the sudden movement damages some items in my cart. Also, some level design problems like little gaps in the wall become more apparent when the cart gets yoinked through them and you are forced to reset. 

Overall, I think the game really just needs more polish which is good because the game is in EA, but right now I would not give it more than 2 stars, there are way too many problems and just a bit of fun.
Dude this is repo in vr i mean just from the trailer you can it

Me and my friend played for a good 20 minutes and it was probably the most fun I’ve had on a vr game just messing around I wish there was a easy way to end the game because I broke everything and had to wait out everything. Overall pretty funny

reviews.review_title: Looks fun but I have issues
reviews.review_description: I’ve seen some videos and clips on this game and it looks super fun and the design is like repo which is cool but I can’t log in the game it just shows the loading screen for 5 minutes while giving tips and then kicks me out.

Solo is rough but multiplayer slaps
Gets wrecked alone but jumps into a public lobby and suddenly it clicks. Needs a tiny bit more polish but the core gameplay is solid

reviews.review_description: This game is really fun and scary and with people the game gets way better. The monsters scare me alot the levels get harder but somehow more fun

Honest opinion, if you don't like a lot of horror this is for you since its funny and scary!
10\10

reviews.review_title: Silly with a mix of YEAH
reviews.review_description: I love this game it really brings out the creeps while having others to share to experience with I recommend playing with friends!
After loading in you have to wait till your done logging and it takes ages you think it's done it but in reality it hasn't fix your game

Hi JA.MO15,

We're a small team still in early access, and we're actively looking into performance issues like this. Could you please join our Discord and look for "AK" or post in the help channel? We'd love to work with you 1 on 1 to figure out what's causing the long loading times on your setup.

Also, if you could let us know which headset you're using, that would help us narrow things down faster.

Thanks for giving us a try anyway, feedback like yours helps us improve the game for everyone.

reviews.review_description: It crashed my headset and no one plays it

developer_response.body: Hi doomfan, So sorry for the crash! 😱 Mind sharing your headset model so we can fix it? Also, try a quick restart if it’s been running a while, that usually helps. We’re still a small crew, so come join our Discord! The devs are often in there to play rounds with everyone. Hope you’ll stick around!
